summary: Iteration 8 (Profile interest chip toggle) end-to-end verified. Backend: 6/6 new tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8.py pass — they assert exact parity between the Profile chip-derived label ('Doubles','Tournaments','Putting') and the Discovery filter keyword ('doubles','tournament','putt') via PUT /api/users/me + GET /api/discovery, including multi-chip selection, label removal, free-text 'tournament partners' regression, and privacy exclusion. Iteration-7 regression suite still 5/5 green. Frontend: signed up TEST_i8ui_* user via UI, opened /profile → Edit. All 5 chips (data-testid=profile-interested-in-tag-{casual|doubles|league|tournament|putt}) render inside data-testid=profile-interested-in-tags. Clicking 'Doubles' wrote 'Doubles' into the input + flipped aria-pressed=true. Clicking 'Putting' produced 'Doubles, Putting' (multi-select works). Toggling Doubles OFF produced 'Putting' (label removed, Putting preserved). Manually typing 'tournament partners, Putting' into the input auto-activated BOTH the Tournaments and Putting chips via substring detection (the bug the user reported is fixed). Save → 'Profile saved successfully!' message rendered, then navigated /feed → /profile → Edit and confirmed Doubles still in the input + chip aria-pressed=true (persistence verified). Navigated to /discovery, clicked the 'Doubles' interest filter chip — deck refetched to 14 cards including users whose interestedIn is exactly the chip label 'Doubles' (visible in card body), proving the user's whole concern is now fixed.
action_items: (Carry-over) 5 seed-dependent tests in test_api.py + test_new_features.py still fail in the full backend suite — pre-existing since iter 3, explicitly out of scope per review request.
critical_code_review_comments: Profile.jsx INTEREST_TAG_OPTIONS values ('casual','doubles','league','tournament','putt') match Discovery.jsx INTEREST_OPTIONS exactly — keep these in lockstep. Consider extracting the shared array into /app/frontend/src/lib/interestTags.js to prevent future drift.

toggleTag() regex `\b${opt.label}\b[,;]?\s*` correctly removes a whole-word label occurrence including trailing comma. Confirmed by test: 'Doubles, Putting' → toggle Doubles off → 'Putting' (no stray comma/whitespace).

activeTags() uses substring (.includes(opt.value)), which is intentional — it lets free-text 'tournament partners' light up the Tournaments chip. Mirrors the backend's case-insensitive regex substring on the server, so what-you-see-on-the-chip == what-the-filter-matches.

Minor UX nit (not a blocker): the chip uses opt.label ('Tournaments' plural) in the text but the keyword stored on Discovery is singular ('tournament'). The substring match makes this work, but if a user manually types 'Tournament' (singular) the chip still activates — which is fine and arguably better.

success_rate.backend: 100% on iteration-8 features (6/6 in test_iteration8.py). Iteration-7 suite still 5/5.
success_rate.frontend: 100% on iteration-8 features — chip render, toggle add, toggle remove, multi-select, free-text auto-detection, save+persist, navigate-away+back persist, Discovery filter parity.

test_credentials: UI signup: TEST_i8ui_<random>@example.com / demo1234 (Firebase Identity Toolkit). Backend tests create TEST_i8v_/TEST_i8chip_* and clean up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-8 selectors on Profile edit form: profile-interested-in-tags (container), profile-interested-in-tag-{casual|doubles|league|tournament|putt} (5 chips with aria-pressed reflecting active state), profile-interested-in-input (existing free-text), profile-interested-in-private (privacy checkbox). Run only iter-8: `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iteration8.py -v`. Pure frontend feature — backend was unchanged; the iter-8 backend tests are integration sanity checks confirming the chip-derived strings flow through PUT /api/users/me and surface under the matching /api/discovery?interested_in= keyword. activeTags() uses substring (not whole word), so free-text 'tournament partners' lights the Tournaments chip — this is intentional. toggleTag() uses whole-word regex `\b<label>\b[,;]?\s*` for removal, preserving custom prose around the label.
rca of the issue: User-reported issue: 'The interests options need to be added to the edit profile page or the search filter won't work.' Root cause was the Discovery chip filter did substring matching on free-typed text — users had no way to know which keywords would match. Fix: added a chip toggle on Profile edit using the EXACT keyword values from Discovery's INTEREST_OPTIONS, with the labels mirroring Discovery's display labels. Verified: (1) chip click writes the label string into interestedIn, (2) saved value persists across navigation, (3) saved label is found by Discovery's case-insensitive substring filter (matches both 'Doubles'→'doubles' and 'Tournaments'→'tournament'), (4) free-text 'tournament partners' auto-activates the Tournaments chip on next edit so the UI stays consistent with whatever the user already saved.
